ab: Aping, ilumaq asu?
en: are you home?

ab: Uu, mulumaqa dii.
en: Yes, come on in.

ab: Aping, ilumaq asu?
en: Api, are you home?

ab: Batu caak, uka Aping lumaqi! alama’aza? nataqu’an zaku Aping ca mucuqaisin.
ch: B:Aping不在家耶!我是Batu，有什麼事嗎? 回來我會轉告Aping。
en: Api is not at home. I am Ipay. What can I do for you? I will let him know when he comes back.

ab: Cima ca amu?
en: Who are you?

ab: qudasnau’az, maca azam a Abis cin Aping, kadangazivang azam matua ki hilavi.
ch: B:奶奶，我們是Abis和Aping，幫我們開門!
en: Grandma, we are Abas and Api, please open the door for us.

ab: Cima ca?
en: Who is it?

ab: Batu azak, munca’anti caak kilim it Ulang.
en: I am Buya, I am here for Utay.

ab: Qudasnau’az, maipati caak tantungu han suan.
en: Grandma, I am here to see you.

ab: Talbuntukin asu nii tu maipati, minasaz azak su’un.
ch: A:你好久沒來了，我好想念你。
en: You haven’t been here for a long time. I missed you so much.

ab: Aping, maipati caak kilim su’un kavila.
en: Api, I am here to play with you.

ab: Mulumaqa dii, talbuntukin azak mantatala su’un.
en: Come on in! I have been waiting for you for a long time.

ab: Sincuaz naak ca hutan dii, tanami ma’un ti.
ch: A:這是我種的地瓜，吃吃看!
en: This is the sweet potato I grow. Try it!

ab: Mantan madavuc ca hutan dii.
en: The sweet potato is so sweet.

ab: Maipa’a dii! sincuaz naak ca kukutdaqun dii, tanami ma’un ti.
ch: A:過來!這是我種的水果，吃看看。
en: Come here! These are the fruits I grow. You should try it.

ab: Madavuc sala ca kukutdaqun dii.
en: The fruit tastes sweet.

ab: Mulungu’avang! tanami ma’un ca sincuaz naak tu tipul dii.
ch: A:請坐! 這是我種的玉米吃看看!
en: Take a seat! You should try some organic corn that I grow.

ab: Uu, mantan madavuc ca tipul dii.
en: Wow! The corn tastes so sweet.

ab: Ma’un asu izuk i?
en: Do you want some oranges?

ab: Nii caak maqtu ma’un is izuk, maqtu’in ca ma’unin i pingku.
ch: B:我不能吃橘子，我吃蘋果就好。
en: I can’t eat oranges. I will have some apples instead.

ab: Ma’un asu ki pang i?
en: Would you want some cookies?

ab: Uu, mantan caak asa.
en: Sure, I like it very much.

ab: Namacala caak kukutdaqun paka’un su’un.
en: Let me peel the fruit for you.

ab: Miqumicang, madanum sala ca kukutdaqun dii.
en: Thank you. This fruit is so juicy.

ab: Siza caak danum paquu su’un.
en: I will bring you some water.

ab: Miqumicang.
en: Thank you.

ab: Pimadai’i ma’un, madia sala ca sanglav sinhanat i naak.
en: Enjoy yourself. I cooked a lot of dishes.

ab: Uu, tutuza tu mantan mahancum, macial kaunun amin.
ch: B:嗚! 真的很香也好吃。
en: Oh! It smells good and looks delicious.

ab: Pimadai’i ma’un, madia’ang sala han dii.
en: Enjoy yourself. There is a lot more.

ab: Nanii caak maz’av ca!
en: I’ll make myself at home then.

ab: Pimadai’i ma’un, madia’ang sala han dii.
en: Enjoy yourself. There are plenty here.

ab: Madia’ing ca sinka’un naak, ni’ing maqtu mauqnang ma’un.
ch: B:我吃了好多，不能再吃了。
en: I ate a lot and couldn’t eat any more.
